Title: Profiling of phytohormone-specific microRNAs and characterization of the miR160-ARF1 module involved in glandular trichome development and artemisinin biosynthesis in Artemisia annua

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimalarial sesquiterpene lactone used for treatment of malaria
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher AaDBR2 expression is associated with increased artemisinin biosynthesis; activation of AaDBR2 promotes artemisinin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaDBR2 expression was reduced in miR160-overexpressing plants and increased in miR160-suppressed plants. 2. AaDBR2 expression was positively regulated by ARF1 through direct promoter binding and activation. 3. AaDBR2 is a key downstream artemisinin pathway gene linking the miR160-ARF1 module to artemisinin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the Δ11(13) double bond of artemisinic aldehyde to form dihydroartemisinic aldehyde in the artemisinin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Dihydroartemisinic acid branch of artemisinin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct precursor branch leading to artemisinin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/branch step supported by regulatory focus on its promoter activation in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves/glandular trichome-related tissues; transcript level responsive to miR160 and ARF1 transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytohormone context of MeJA, SA, and ABA in study background
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AaDBR2 promoter used in Y1H and dual-LUC reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/qPCR expression analysis in miR160 and ARF1 transgenic lines; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-LUC assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising pathway target for metabolic engineering; stacking with positive regulator ARF1 or relieving miR160 repression could enhance artemisinin production.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics; small RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA756118); degradome sequencing (BioProject PRJNA756886)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only AaDBR2, not AaADS, showed direct ARF1 promoter binding in Y1H/EMSA/dual-LUC assays.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimalarial sesquiterpene lactone used for treatment of malaria
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates glandular trichome development and artemisinin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-responsive negative regulator linking phytohormone signals to artemisinin output through ARF1 cleavage.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of artemisinin biosynthesis indirectly via miRNA-guided cleavage of a positive transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-transcriptional RNA cleavage/silencing of ARF1 mRNA
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses ARF1, thereby indirectly reducing AaDBR2 and also lowering AaADS, AaCYP71AV1, and AaALDH1 transcript levels
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially responsive to MeJA and SA; high abundance among sequenced miRNAs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed after phytohormone treatment; overexpressed in 35S:miR160 lines and reduced in STTM160 lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter for overexpression; STTM160 under 35S for repression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Small RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; overexpression; STTM silencing; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; glandular trichome phenotyping; RLM-5' RACE; transient YFP reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppressing miR160 is a candidate strategy to increase glandular trichome density and artemisinin yield in A. annua.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: miR160 overexpression caused defective glandular trichomes and lower artemisinin; broader developmental effects are possible
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics; small RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA756118); degradome sequencing (BioProject PRJNA756886)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR160 was identified as a phytohormone-responsive candidate from integrated transcriptome, sRNA, and degradome analyses. 2. miR160 overexpression decreased glandular trichome density and reduced artemisinin content by 40%–70%, whereas STTM160 increased artemisinin by 65%–81%. 3. miR160 directly cleaves ARF1 mRNA and thereby suppresses the positive ARF1–AaDBR2 regulatory axis.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimalarial sesquiterpene lactone used for treatment of malaria
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates glandular trichome development and artemisinin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive transcriptional activator directly connecting auxin-responsive regulation to a key artemisinin pathway gene, AaDBR2.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AaDBR2 directly; overexpression also elevates AaADS, AaCYP71AV1, and AaALDH1 transcript levels
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Inverse to miR160 after phytohormone treatments
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in 35S:miR160 plants and increased in STTM160 plants; elevated in ARF1-OVX and reduced in ARF1-RNAi lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter for overexpression/YFP fusion; native AaDBR2 promoter used for target assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RLM-5' RACE; transient YFP reporter assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; RNAi; overexpression; miR160-resistant mutant overexpression; HPLC/LC-MS metabolite profiling; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate positive regulator for engineering high-artemisinin cultivars; miR160-resistant ARF1 allele gave larger gains than wild-type ARF1 overexpression.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broader auxin-related developmental effects may occur, though not deeply characterized here
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics; small RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA756118); degradome sequencing (BioProject PRJNA756886)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARF1 was validated as a direct miR160 cleavage target by RLM-5' RACE and transient reporter assays. 2. ARF1-RNAi reduced artemisinin by 18%–44%, while ARF1 overexpression increased artemisinin by 61%–94%; a miR160-resistant ARFm1 increased artemisinin by 168.8%–246.2%. 3. ARF1 directly binds the D2/AuxRE motif of the AaDBR2 promoter and activates its expression, thereby promoting artemisinin biosynthesis and glandular trichome development.
